column2: sīcut mons Siōnis~; nōn commovēbitur in aeternum
column3: quī habitat in Jerūsalīme. ^ They that trust in the Lord shall be as mount Sion~; he shall not be moved for ever

column2: et Dominus in circumitū populī suī
column3: ex hōc nunc et usque in saeculum. ^ Mountains are round about it
column4: and the Lord is round about his people
column5: from henceforth now and for ever.

column1: 3 ^ Quia nōn relinquet Dominus virgam peccātōrum super sortem jūstōrum
column2: ut nōn extendant jūstī ad inīquitātem manūs suās. ^ For the Lord will not leave the rod of sinners upon the lot of the just
column3: that the just may not stretch forth their hands to iniquity.

column7: and to the upright of heart.

column2: addūcet Dominus cum operantibus inīquitātem. Pax super Isrāhēlem. ^ But such as turn aside into bonds
column3: the Lord shall lead out with the workers of iniquity. Peace upon Israel.
